raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 346 aims to optimize the impact of Low-Income Housing Tax Credits and maximize the production of affordable homes in California.",
    "AB 346 allows the Tax Credit Allocation Committee (TCAC) to pair state tax credits with either 4% or 9% federal credits.",
    "AB 346 does not impose any new taxes, as the amount of credits is predetermined in the budget each year.",
    "Almost all new affordable rental housing in California is financed through federal Low-Income Housing Tax Credits (LIHTC).",
    "The 9% credits are allocated by TCAC, while developers are eligible for the 4% credits if they finance at least 50% of the development's cost with tax-exempt bonds.",
    "Tax-exempt bonds and 4% credits have become oversubscribed, leading to unmet demand and uncertainty for bond applicants in California.",
    "AB 346 grants TCAC the authority to move credits to the unconstrained 9% federal tax credit program when tax-exempt bonds are oversubscribed.",
    "AB 346 is expected to increase what corporate investors will pay for each dollar of state credit by roughly 7 cents.",
    "AB 346 will result in an additional $42 million in private investment per year to help address California's affordable housing crisis."
  ]
}
```
